titleStmt.title: Letter from W.M. Connolly to the Magistrates, Petty Sessions, Templemore, 22 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: W.M. Connolly

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: The letter is a directive from the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland and sent by W J. Connolly in Dublin Castle to the Templemore Magistrate's Office. The letter request information on a judgement in the case of Richard Carroll, sentenced to six months with hard labour for assaulting his wife. Enclosed is a petition by Connolly, requesting that his sentence be revoked as there is nobody else to look after his wife and children, and his prisoner record.Ivor Churchill Guest, 1st Viscount Wimborne (1873-1939) was appointed the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland in 1915. Guest worked closely alongside Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) in the administration of Ireland. Following the suppression of the Rising, Guest, under pressure, was forced to resign as Lord Lieutenant. A later inquiry into the Rising exonerated Guest and held Augustine Birrell (1850-1933) chiefly responsible for the events that occurred. Guest would continue to serve as Lord Lieutenant until 1918.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Form 36 Dublin Castle 22nd April 1916 GENTLEMEN, I am directed by the Lord Lieutenant to transmit a Memorial in behalf
of Richard Carroll
and to request you will report the facts of the case, and favour me with your
opinion thereon for His Excellency's information, and with your reasons for any
recommendation you may desire to make. If a Fine has been imposed, you will be so good as to state :
(1.) To whom it has been awarded, and
(2.) If paid, whether any portion of it has been paid over to the party to
whom it was awarded. I have the honour to be,
Gentlemen,
Your obedient Servant, W.M. Connolly N.B.âPapers to be returned.
The Magistrates
at Petty Sessions
Templemore
Petition from a person undergoing Imprisonment with hard labour Limerick Prison Date: 20 April, 1916 To His Excellency the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland. The Petition of 130 Richard Carroll Humbly Sheweth I was conviced
at Templemore petty session and
senticed to six caldren
months with hard labour
for assulting my wife
would you conedder my case
and take some of my
sentence off as my wife
and three children as nobody
ells to look after them
but me and you petitioner as
in duty bound will everpray Richard Carroll
Form 17.
A.D.
Form to be attached to all Petitions from Prisoners (In cases of convicts a copy of the convict's photograph should accompany the first petition after reception into Convict Prison.) Date of previous Petition,
Decision on Previous Petition, Name and Reg. No., - - -Richard Carroll. 130/16
Age on conviction - - - 24 years
If in custody before trial state date of
committal for trial, ----Was not in custody
If bailed before trial, state date of discharge â
Date of conviction, - - -5 th April 1916
Place of conviction and name of tribunal, Templemore Petty Sessions
Before whom tried, - - -M.Hornibrook Esq R.W.
Whether pleaded guilty or not guilty, - Convicted summarily
Crime, - - Aggravated assault on his
wife - Sarah Carroll.
Sentence, - - -6 cal. moths HL
& 3 cal.months or Bail} consec.
Date from which sentence takes effect, - 5 April 1916 (warrant to commit
in default of bail
not yet lodged.)
Date of termination of sentence of im-
prisonment, - - - 4 October 1916
Date when eligible for release on licence,
or on remission of sentence if well
conducted in meantime, - 5 th Septr. 1916
If fined, state to whom fine is payable, - no fine imposed
Previous character and circumstances, - 3 times previously convicted
Circumstances unknown. PREVIOUS CONVICTIONS.
(The previous convictions will only be given in connection with the first petition after each conviction. With subsequent
petitions the number of previous convictions will be summarised and a reference made by date to the first petition.)
Date. Offence. Sentence.
21.4.15 Steal a wooden post value s1/0 7 Days Impt or 5/
15.9.15 Drunk &Disorderly I 14 Days or 12/-
' ' ' malicious damage to
door of Lock-up 11 14 Days or 13/-
consec If convicted jointly with other prisoners,
state names and sentences of such
prisoners Convicted alone Conduct in Prison Satisfactory.
Report of Governor as to truth
of statements contained in
petition,&c Statements as to offence, sentence
& place of conviction correct. Truth of other
statement unknown by me. W Webb Chief Warder
for Governor. sick H.M.Prison, Limerick Date, 21 April 1916 State of Health
(If the prisoner be under medical
treatment, please specify the
disease, state whether it is
of a serious character, and
whether his life is in danger
or likely to be endangered
by confinement) Health
W Watt
Medical Officer
